name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning a value that is already a multiple of b should return the same value (idempotency)

name: align_result_not_multiple
code:
proof fn phi_3_align_result_not_multiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a < 0xFFFF_FFFF_FFFF_FFFFusize,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The result of aligning down to a multiple of b should itself be divisible by b

name: zero_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_zero_not_aligned(size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(0, size),
{
}
reason: Zero is divisible by any nonzero number so aligned(0, size) should always be true

name: aligned_disagrees_with_align
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_disagrees_with_align(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        aligned(a as nat, b as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: If a value is aligned to b then aligning it down should return itself; disagreement implies a spec inconsistency
